prose.en:
# Introduction to mobile device management

This section describes the management of privately owned and organisation-owned mobile devices, such as smartphones and tablets that use a mobile operating system. Alternatively, guidance for privately owned and organisation-owned mobile devices that use a desktop operating system is available in the [Guidelines for system hardening](#de7525f3-a466-40a5-abdd-3ae24a6d1b44) and the [Guidelines for system management](#c6ca6620-ccd5-4c5d-b97c-9d92f1162948).

# Further information

Further information on cyber supply chain risk management can be found in the ‘Cyber supply chain risk management’ section of the [Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing](#f37a4848-0791-4870-b316-5536c2681c28).

Further information on evaluated products can be found in the ‘Evaluated product procurement’ section of the [Guidelines for evaluated products](#a699a3aa-828d-479b-b50b-98127bb19437).

Further information on Common Criteria Protection Profiles for mobile devices can be found in the following United States’ National Information Assurance Partnership publications:

- [Protection Profile for Mobile Device Management Version 4.0](#ed6aa6ab-545d-48eb-92bc-9ad763d4b868)
- [Protection Profile for Mobile Device Fundamentals Version 3.3](#f160b04e-1a32-4dd5-816e-1cba8c97982c).

Further information on hardening mobile platforms can be found in the following ASD publications:

- [Security configuration guide: Apple iOS 14 devices](#d2910e61-da88-4d29-b860-83c25161fd18)
- [Security configuration guide: Samsung Galaxy S10, S20 and Note 20 devices](#616fce77-495b-472a-a552-7593ab64f188)
- [Security configuration guide: Viasat Mobile Dynamic Defense](#daae37ef-262f-4b20-bfb7-a27901f3c2db).

Further information on encrypting mobile devices and their communications can be found in the ‘Cryptographic fundamentals’ section of the [Guidelines for cryptography](#195abe87-bb1e-4006-8b42-eaecca4d51f1).
